revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=善蔵塚古墳| 所在地=福岡県八女郡広川町六田字善蔵塚| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長95m| 築造年代=6世紀前半～中頃| 出土品=埴輪| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]|}}
'''善蔵塚古墳'''（ぜんぞうづかこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女郡]][[広川町]]に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。
八女古墳群における有力古墳であり、[[岩戸山古墳]]、[[石人山古墳]]に次ぎ第3位の規模を誇る。前方部を西に向け、2段築成の墳丘には[[葺き石]]と[[埴輪]]が巡る<ref>「日本古墳大辞典」318頁</ref>。
築造時期は表採された[[円筒埴輪]]などから、[[6世紀]]前半～中頃とみられ<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm 善蔵塚古墳 広川町]</ref>、[[乗場古墳]]と同時期かやや遅れ、[[鶴見山古墳]]と並行すると考えられる<ref>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」9頁</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] {{DEFAULTSORT:せんそうつかこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:広川町 (福岡県)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Zenzōzuka Kofun| 画像=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th cen..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Zenzōzuka Kofun| 画像=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

[[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirokawa, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[ja:善蔵塚古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Zenzōzuka Kofun| 画像=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Zenzōzuka Kofun| 画像=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}}

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references />

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references />

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}}

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}}

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent burial mounds within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| address={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-typed tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Zenzōzuka Kofun| image=[[File:Zenzozuka kofun in Hirokawa.jpg|250px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|02.4|N|130|34|04.2|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: 95 meters| 築造年代=Early to mid-6th century| 出土品=Haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a National other_designation in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| }}
'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}}

'''Zenzōzuka Kofun''' (善蔵塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
This kofun is one of the prominent Kofuns within the Yame Kofun Group, ranking third in size after {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}} and {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The mound faces west and features a two-tiered construction adorned with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺き石}} (paving stones) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.<ref>「Nihon Kofun Daijiten」p.318</ref>
Based on surface-collected cylindrical haniwa fragments, the construction of Zenzōzuka Kofun is estimated to date from the early to mid-6th century.<ref>[http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/konnamachi/accessmap/popupbunkazai/popbunka_9.htm Zenzōzuka Kofun, Hirokawa Town]</ref> It is considered contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} and slightly postdates the {{ill|Nobaba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.9.</ref>
